title: Kitchenware - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Kitchenware 21 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV BETHLEHEM HOUSE OF BREAD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping style food pantry focusing on culturally relevant food and options for those with food allergies, sensitivities, and other dietary or lifestyle preferences such as vegan or vegetarian. Caring and Sharing Store provides, when available, clothing (infant-adult sizes), cleaning supplies, kitchenware (plates, bowls, utensils), personal hygiene supplies (toilet paper, shampoo, toothbrushes, female hygiene products), bedding, and laundry supplies. Also provides a seasonal community garden with 24 raised garden beds available to the public. Accepts donations. 9055 SW Locust Street Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Food Pantry: Sunday/Thursday 1pm-3pm, Tuesday 4pm-6pm Caring and Sharing Store: Sunday/Thursday 1pm-3pm Community Garden: Season opens Sunday, May 11, 2024: Reservation required Donations: Tuesday-Friday 9am-4pm, Sunday 1pm-3pm Email (503) 639-3181 1 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS CLUBS OF PORTLAND METROPOLITAN AREA INUKAI CLUB COMMUNITY CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a community closet with clothes, shoes, canned goods, home goods, and hygiene supplies. 560 SE 3rd Avenue Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Unrestricted. No membership needed. Hours: By appointment only (503) 640-4558 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT TOD'S CORNER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, household goods (including kitchen items), bedding and linens, and other items. Accepts donations of men's clothing, kitchenware, small appliances, bedding and linens, and bathroom supplies. 10202 SE 32nd Avenue Suite 601 Milwaukie, OR 97222 Eligibility: Individuals diagnosed with HIV who have a monthly income below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level Hours: Monday 1pm-4pm, Tuesday/Thursday 2pm-5pm, Wednesday 1pm-3pm Email (503) 349-4699 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS SERVICE CENTER PERSONAL GOODS AND DIAPERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, hygiene items as available, and household goods, including bedding and kitchenware. Diapers are available through PDX Diaper Bank. Accepts donations of gently used clothing and household items. 8800 SE 80th Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Clothing and Hygiene supplies: Tuesday-Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 771-7914 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FURNISH HOPE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a furniture bank of new and gently used household items, including kitchen basics such as dishes, glassware, bake ware, small appliances, utensils, pot, pans, home furniture, bedding, and cleaning supplies. 1006 SE 9th Street Bend, OR 97702 Eligibility: Varies by referral partner, but household must meet at least one of the following: -Individuals transitioning out of homelessness or recovery -People with disabilities or medical challenges -Refugees and people fleeing domestic violence -Foster youth and families, and aged-out foster teenagers -Veteran in the household -Families who have lost a home to a natural disaster Hours: Monday-Saturday 9am-11am (541) 638-0657 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GG'S HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides blankets, a preselected bag of food, clothing, diapers, household items, cleaning supplies, and toiletries. Other items may be available when donations are received. Accepts donations of similar items and monetary donations. 1714 Pacific Avenue Forest Grove, OR 97116 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-1pm Email (503) 359-3871 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS RESOURCES HELPING HANDS RESOURCES CLOTHING ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, shoes, bedding, household items, and clothing for babies and newborns. Items are subject to availability. 1755 13th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday/Saturday 9am-noon, closed holidays Email (503) 364-9936 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JEFFERSON COUNTY FAITH BASED NETWORK LINC MADRAS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides household goods, budgeting, job, and life skills mentoring, and connections to local food banks and community meals. May also assist with rent, electric, and propane vouchers when available. Assistance is limited and not guaranteed. Utility assistance is available once per year. 278 SE 8th Street Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Wednesday 10am-2pm Email (541) 475-4446 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC NEWBERG GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people with local churches and service agencies. Basic goods and services are offered to clients as they are donated. Services available may include, but are not limited to: bicycles, breakfast/lunch bags, clothing closets (adults and children), community meals, counseling services, diapers, haircuts, H.U.G.S. (hats, umbrellas, gloves, scarves, and socks), kitchen supplies, laundry service, bedding/linens, personal grooming supplies/toiletries, over-the-counter medications, personal care kits, physical therapy, showers, water bill assistance, and firewood. Accepts donations of bedding and linen, clothing, and personal grooming supplies. 200 S College Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 537-3999 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF BENTON COUNTY GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides children's clothing, personal hygiene items, linens (bedding, towels, etc.), baby bundles (newborn baby supplies and clothing), kitchen supplies, and a gift closet (for when community members can't afford to purchase gifts for special occasions). 4900 NW Hwy 99 Suite 102 Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Low-income community members in need Hours: Helpline: Monday-Thursday 9:30am-1:30pm Email (541) 757-8111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY GAP RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides furniture, kitchen items, firewood, medical equipment, personal hygiene supplies, cleaning supplies, and bedding/linens. Accepts donations of similar items. Assistance depends on availability. Follow-ups may be provided once the need is met. Eligibility: Low-income households (self-declared or referred and verified by caseworker, employer, or landlord) Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 650-0153 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF GREATER HILLSBORO GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides essential household items, including beds and bedding, linens, furniture, clothing, diapers, hygiene products, cleaning and laundry supplies, paper goods, and work boots. Donations are accepted of the items the agency distributes, with an ongoing need for couches, kitchen tables, and queen-size or smaller beds (including mattresses, box springs, and frames). Referrals to local food pantries are available. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday noon-2:30pm, Tuesday/Thursday 9:30am-noon Email (503) 917-0205 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF LINN COUNTY GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Basic goods are offered to clients as they are donated. Goods available may include, but are not limited to household items (cleaning supplies, laundry detergent, toilet paper, and paper towels), personal care supplies (such as toothbrushes and shampoo), durable medical goods, diapers and wipes, linens, towels and blankets, kitchenware (dishes, pots and pans, glasses, etc), and baby blankets. Connects people in need with local churches and service agencies. No financial assistance is available. 1606 Santiam Road SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Low income individuals and families Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Thursday 9am-1pm, Tuesday 5pm-8pm Email (541) 924-5683 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF TIGARD, TUALATIN, AND SHERWOOD GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, hygiene supplies, household goods, bedding/linens, over-the counter medicines and supplies, financial training, assistance with resume writing, and nutrition courses. Requests are screened and referred to outside agencies for fulfillment. Assistance is dependent upon available funding and not guaranteed. Also accepts donations of bedding/linens and personal grooming supplies. 10900 SW 121st Avenue Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Wednesday 9:30am-12:30pm Email (503) 443-6888 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARLA'S MALL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a thrift store with books, toys, household items, new hygiene products, knick-knacks, and clothing including interview or work-appropriate attire. Offers volunteer work experience and credit recovery for students working at the store. Also accepts donations of new and gently used items. 2500 E Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Thrift Store: Unrestricted Volunteers: Baker School District high school students Hours: School year: Monday-Thursday 8am-11am and 1pm-4pm, closed holidays. Hours are subject to change. Summer hours: June 16th-July 17th Monday-Thursday 8:30am-12:30pm Email (541) 524-2309 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROSEBURG DREAM CENTER HOUSEHOLD GOODS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides dishes, cookware, towels, sheets, blankets, and decor when available. Also accepts donations of these items. 2555 NE Diamond Lake Boulevard Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday 10am-2pm and 4pm-7pm, Tuesday-Thursday 10am-2pm Email (541) 673-5918 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL LA PINE SOCIAL SERVICES OFFICE SOCIAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency food boxes, clothing for adults, household items (kitchenware and small appliances), prescription assistance, gas money (for job interviews or appointments), emergency motel vouchers, and ID assistance. 51484 Morson Road La Pine, OR 97739 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-noon and 12:30pm-2pm Email (541) 536-6135 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL LEBANON CONFERENCE FOOD PANTRY AND CLOTHING CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a food pantry, providing a 3 day supply of food. Also has a rotating supply of clothing, shoes, furniture, appliances, and other household goods supplied by donations. Food, clothing items, and household items accepted for donation. 100 Main Street Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Must meet federal poverty guidelines Hours: Wednesday 9am-11am and 11:30am-3pm Email (541) 258-5405 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY LINDHOLM SOCIAL SERVICE OFFICE EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, household items, small appliances, furniture, beds, propane vouchers for heating, emergency prescription assistance, and bus tokens when available. Assistance depends on the zip code. 456 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-4pm by appointment only Email (541) 689-6747 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SWEET HOME EMERGENCY MINISTRIES SHEM FOOD PANTRY AND CARMEN'S CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an emergency food box to individuals and families in need once per month. Each food box contains enough food for each family member to eat 3 meals per day for 3-4 days. Clients may obtain fresh bread and produce weekly in addition to their monthly food box. Carmen's Closet provides clothing, hygiene items, and small household goods. 1115 Long Street Sweet Home, OR 97386 Eligibility: Must meet USDA income guidelines Hours: Tuesday/Wednesday/Saturday 9am-11:30am, closed 2nd Saturday of the month Email (541) 367-6504 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TILLAMOOK ADVENTIST COMMUNITY SERVICES HERALD CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping-style food pantry and a thrift shop that offers bedding, small household items, personal hygiene supplies, and clothing. Offers community gardening opportunities. Accepts donations for the food pantry and thrift shop. 2710 First Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Food Pantry and Thrift Shop: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm Donation Drop-off: Tuesday/Thursday 8:30am-2pm Email (503) 842-4794 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BETHLEHEM HOUSE OF BREAD PANTRY BOYS & GIRLS CLUBS OF PORTLAND METROPOLITAN AREA COMMUNITY CLOSET CASCADE AIDS PROJECT TOD'S CORNER CLACKAMAS SERVICE CENTER PERSONAL GOODS AND DIAPERS FURNISH HOPE FURNISH HOPE GG'S HOUSE GG'S HOUSE HELPING HANDS RESOURCES HELPING HANDS RESOURCES CLOTHING ASSISTANCE JEFFERSON COUNTY FAITH BASED NETWORK LINC MADRAS LOVE INC NEWBERG GAP MINISTRIES LOVE INC OF BENTON COUNTY GAP MINISTRIES LOVE INC OF LINN COUNTY GAP MINISTRIES LOVE INC OF TIGARD, TUALATIN, AND SHERWOOD GAP MINISTRIES MARLA'S MALL MARLA'S MALL ROSEBURG DREAM CENTER HOUSEHOLD GOODS ST VINCENT DE PAUL LA PINE SOCIAL SERVICES OFFICE SOCIAL SERVICES ST VINCENT DE PAUL LEBANON CONFERENCE FOOD PANTRY AND CLOTHING CLOSET ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY EMERGENCY SERVICES SWEET HOME EMERGENCY MINISTRIES SHEM FOOD PANTRY AND CARMEN'S CLOSET TILLAMOOK ADVENTIST COMMUNITY SERVICES HERALD CENTER
